svg.text: A parameter scan finds stable periods directly, then the superstable points track how the doubling cascade compresses.
Detected stable periods on a direct r-scan
negative Lyapunov plus a repeating tail makes the windows explicit

text.tspan: otherwise count the point as chaos

svg.text: the critical point x = 0.5 lands back on itself at each band center
gap ratios already lean toward the Feigenbaum constant

text.tspan: Top: direct period detection on a dense r-scan. Bottom left: superstable points where the orbit of 0.5 lands back on 0.5.
Bottom right: successive gap ratios already drift toward δ, even in this small public calculation.
